## Overview

Ordnance Survey (OS) is Great Britain's national mapping agency, providing authoritative geospatial data and APIs. The OS Places API provides UK address lookup, geocoding, and UPRN matching, available free to public sector organisations under the Public Sector Geospatial Agreement (PSGA).

## Products & Services

| Product | Category | Relevance to Project |
|---------|----------|---------------------|
| OS Places API | Address Lookup / Geocoding | Forecourt address validation, postcode-to-coordinate geocoding |
| OS Names API | Place Name Search | Place name resolution for citizen search |
| AddressBase Premium | Address Data | Underlying data behind OS Places API |

## Pricing Model

- Free for UK public sector under PSGA (Public Sector Geospatial Agreement)
- Commercial pricing available for private sector consumers
- Rate limits: 50 RPM (development), 600 RPM (live)
- Data updated every 6 weeks

## UK Government Presence

- G-Cloud listed: N/A (free under PSGA for public sector)
- UK data centres: Yes
- Operated by: Ordnance Survey (UK Government-owned trading fund)
- Recommended by: data.gov.uk, API Catalogue (api.gov.uk)

## Compliance

| Standard | Status |
|----------|--------|
| UK GDPR | Compliant |
| UK data residency | Yes |
| PSGA | Covered |

## Strengths

- Authoritative UK address data (AddressBase Premium)
- UPRN-based matching -- UK Government standard for property identification
- Free under PSGA for central government
- REST API with well-documented endpoints
- 6-weekly data updates ensure currency

## Weaknesses

- Rate limits (600 RPM live) may constrain high-frequency geocoding
- 6-week update cycle means new addresses have a lag
- Not suitable for real-time proximity queries at scale (use PostGIS instead)

## Projects Referenced In

- UK Fuel Price Transparency Service (Project 001) -- address validation and geocoding
